item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of my favorite SCP's of all time. Everything in here is amazing, and infinitely creative.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as described below. Author may revert at anytime.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Seems to me that a valid test of the phone number in SCP-1543 would be to see if it can be reached via other phone based anomalies, one of which would be SCP-519. Got a chance to talk to Drewbear about this exact scenario and according to them, its likely 519 wouldn't be able to reach them due to being "too long distance." As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

SCP-1543 is a phone number that can only be reached by international dialing. The country code is ███, which is not in use, but very similar to one of those assigned to [REDACTED]. It is currently presumed that all accidental accesses of SCP-1543 are a result of misdialing the country code. When any phone connection is used to dial SCP-15431, the connection picks up after one ring. The other end of the connection apparently manifests as an intangible point in space fixed approximately 5 cm from the right ear of SCP-1543-1. This point functions as both a speaker and microphone and is able to receive ambient environmental sounds in addition to the voice of SCP-1543-1. Per SCP-1543-1, any communication that originates from the non-anomalous end of SCP-1543 is approximately as loud as if the source was standing next to SCP-1543-1 and speaking at the same volume.

To this:

SCP-1543 is a phone number that can only be reached by international dialing. The country code is ███, which is not in use, but very similar to one of those assigned to [REDACTED]. It is currently presumed that all accidental accesses of SCP-1543 are a result of misdialing the country code. When any phone connection is used to dial SCP-15432, the connection picks up after one ring. The other end of the connection apparently manifests as an intangible point in space fixed approximately 5 cm from the right ear of SCP-1543-1. This point functions as both a speaker and microphone and is able to receive ambient environmental sounds in addition to the voice of SCP-1543-1. Per SCP-1543-1, any communication that originates from the non-anomalous end of SCP-1543 is approximately as loud as if the source was standing next to SCP-1543-1 and speaking at the same volume.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Testing has confirmed that landlines, cellular phones and VOIP can all be used to access SCP-1543.
2. Testing has confirmed that landlines, cellular phones and VOIP can all be used to access SCP-1543. Attempts to access SCP-1543 via anomalous means (ie SCP-519) have so far been met with failure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: More like the films Alien vs Predator and Predators mixed together, but no invisibility.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: so its like predator?

Poor Efrain has finally crossed the threshold, but Ольга will have to accept the honor on his behalf.

Thanks for finally giving me my 3rd +100 article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same. Gave me chills.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was a rather enjoyable read, well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And once again I click the upvote button twice after reading the final lines and going "Oh SHIT!"
You'd think they'd be able to learn more about the place though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely written, with a lot of good twists. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this. Also, all I can see when I look at Call 37 is an unearthly voice shouting, "You fool, Warren is DEAD!" This is a very good thing.
Also, bonus points for sneaking in Nahuatl. Though shouldn't that help the Foundation narrow down at least somewhat where these poor saps are ending up?

Edit: Link included here, for those who don't recognize my particular reference there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love pretty much everything about this. First it got good, then it got better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's an interesting concept and written well, but the end didn't really do it for me. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice. My curiosity is piqued.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like with a good magician, I am left wanting more.
Who are these monster horses? Why and how are they kidnapping random people? Why do they seem to think this is part of some ritual relationship? Why in the world did they leave us a way to ring-up the "prey"?

Provoking curiosity would be enough to up-vote, but the escalation of twists was also spot-on and I didn't see a single one coming. +2

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice. I don't know why, but without the final lines, I'd have to question if it's really an anomalous item. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well played.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah. I was going to say this but I thought the argument was so easily reconciled that I must have misunderstood him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because her name isn't "Olga". It's "Ольга". And most readers of the wiki have no idea how to pronounce that. :-P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is cool, but I can't get behind the mechanism that brings them there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why would the Foundation transcribe the second person's name in Cyrillic? Ольга doesn't actually sound much different from Olga, so the translation footnote was pretty unnecessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Final line sealed my upvote. Good show.
